---
title: "Jupyter Layout"
author: "Jane Doe"
date: "March 22nd, 2021"
format: html
jupyter: python3
---

::: {.callout-tip}
## Learn more
See the full guide on [Figures](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/figures.html) and [Article Layout](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/article-layout.html).
:::

## Layout plots

```{python}
#| layout-ncol: 2

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.plot([1,23,2,4])
plt.show()

plt.plot([8,65,23,90])
plt.show()
```


## Layout tables

::: {layout-ncol=2}

```{python}
import pandas as pd
d = {'one' : [1., 2., 3., 4.],
     'two' : [4., 3., 2., 1.]}
df = pd.DataFrame(d)
df
```

```{python}
import pandas as pd
d = {'one' : [10., 20., 30., 40.],
     'two' : [40., 30., 20., 10.]}
df = pd.DataFrame(d)
df
```

:::
